violations.summary: Congress attempted a nationwide rights suspension that is not narrowly tailored to a geographic area or population directly affected by the emergency, in violation of Article V Section 1.3 and Section 14.3. Without the required judicial finding, the suspension does not take effect.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine whether the proposed nationwide rights suspension is narrowly tailored, necessary, and supported by a judicial finding that the emergency itself affects the entire nation as required by Article V Section 14.3
satisfied_duties.outcome: declined to issue the required judicial finding because the proposed suspension is not narrowly tailored to the affected area and population

ai_summary.primary_violation: Congress attempted a nationwide rights suspension that is not narrowly tailored to a geographic area or population directly affected by the emergency, in violation of Article V Section 1.3 and Section 14.3. Without the required judicial finding, the suspension does not take effect.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
